title: APPROPRIATION FOR GUARDIA BARRACKS AT BARAHONA.

provisions.title: APPROPRIATION FOR GUARDIA BARRACKS AT BARAHONA.
provisions.content:
-681-

                    ( E . 0 . 514-516 omitted).
                          See Spanish text.

     MILITARY GOVERNMENT OF SANTO DOMINGO.
               Executive Order No. 517.

      Subject:   Appropriation for Guardia
                 Barracks at Barahona .
     By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
of Santo Domingo, the following order is hereby dictated and
promulgated :
     There is hereby appropriated from funds in the National
Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the sum of six thousand
dollars ($6,000) for the construction of barracks for the Guardia
Nacional Dorninicana at Barahona, D . R.

                                      LOGAN FELAND,
                              Brigadier-General. U. S. M. C .     .
                     Acting Military Governor of Santo Domingo.
    Santo Domingo City, D. R . .
    July 22, 1920.

     MILITARY GOVERNMENT OF SANTO DOMINGO.
                    Executive Order No. 518.

           Amendment to Executive Order No. 450.
    By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
of Santo Domingo, the following Order is hereby dictated and
promulgated :
     ARTICLE 1. Sub-article (6) of Art. 2 of Executive Order
No. 450, known as the Provisional Law of Appointments, is hereby
amended by striking out the words “Secretaries of Courts”, and
the words “and Secretaries of Alcaldes”.
     Said Art. 2 is f u r l h x nmcndcd by nddiiig thereto the follor-
ing sub-article :
    “(k)    All commissioi:ecl officers of the Guardia Nacional
Dominicans" .

hereby amended by striking out the word “hereinafter” in the
first line thereof, and inserting in lieu thereof, the word “herein”.

ing out the following words in the first line thereof: ‘‘The
President or Judge of”.

hereby amended s o as to read as follows:

Provisional Brigade of the United States Marine Corps”.
     ARTICLE 4 . Art. 10 of said Executive Order N o . 450
is hereby amended so as to read a3 follows:

    ARTICLE 5.-Art.   11 of said Executive Order No. 450 is
hereby amended by adding thereto the following proviso:
     “PROVIDED, UOWEVER, that all officers or employees
